content: Ratification provenance records what was proposed by the agent, not the user's confirmation text. The ratification classifier extracts what_was_ratified from the agent turn and verifies the user turn is confirmatory. raw_user: 'yes' is useless as audit trail — the write records the extraction. Context is loaded once per session, not per pair. Pairs in the same session share a consistent Octowiki snapshot.

provenance.raw_agent: When a 'yes' triggers a write, raw_user: 'yes' is almost useless as audit trail. What you actually want is: { type: 'ratification', provenance: { raw_agent: '...', raw_user: 'yes', what_was_ratified: '<extracted from agent turn>' } }. On the context loading question — load once per session, not per pair.
provenance.raw_user: Yes, the shoemakers project was more for reference about agents as pure functions operating in a behaviour tree. We don't need to copy, but it had quite a good approach. Pairs question is interesting, a usecase is when the agent asks me something, and I just say 'yes'. My answer has no information except to confirm the information the agent said
provenance.what_was_ratified: Ratification provenance records what_was_ratified extracted from the agent turn, not the user's confirmation text. Context loaded once per session, not per pair.
